methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company founded by Stephen Fitzpatrick that has raised $50M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of five private transportation companies—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available. This scarcity of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers constrains valuation precision.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable to this target. The transportation sector contains five potential comparables, but filtering for stage alignment, adjacency, or valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any meaningful subset. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of transportation subsectors and company maturity. No other triangulation methods are documented as feasible given the data constraints.

The absence of sufficient comparable data and the lack of documented cash flows, revenue, or other fundamental metrics leaves the analysis unable to support a specific valuation range. The key uncertainties are structural: the thinness of the peer set, the wide dispersion in maturity and business model among listed comparables, and missing operational or financial data on the target itself. Any valuation conclusion would require either (1) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent sectors, (2) collection of financial or operational metrics from the target and comps, or (3) reliance on asset-based or discounted cash flow methods for which inputs are not provided here.
